_candidateMcpServers._README: DISABLED / UNVETTED. Entries here are NOT launched (only servers under 'mcpServers' are). 'meta-ads-read' is the community token-based Meta read server candidate. To enable: (1) vet the package + pin a known-good version, (2) confirm the token env-var name it expects, (3) move the 'meta-ads-read' object into 'mcpServers' above, then (4) set META_READER_BACKEND=mcp. It authenticates with the existing long-lived META_ACCESS_TOKEN (no OAuth). Reads only; writes always use the direct Graph client.
_candidateMcpServers._candidate: meta-ads-mcp-server@1.5.1 (npm) — token-based, read-only tool set by default, no auto-pagination (provides meta_ads_fetch_pagination_url). UNVETTED: operator must review the package and pin before enabling.
_candidateMcpServers._TODO: vet and pin meta-ads-mcp-server@<reviewed-version>; confirm its token env var (candidate expects META_ADS_ACCESS_TOKEN, mapped below from META_ACCESS_TOKEN).
_candidateMcpServers._meta_suite_note: 'meta-suite' is OUR OWN custom Meta MCP server (distinct from the community 'meta-ads-read' read candidate above). It is a scaffold today: it exposes only the 'server_info' health tool and makes ZERO live Meta calls. Parked here (NOT launched) until the read/write tool tickets (mcp-read-tools / mcp-guarded-write-tools) land. Launch it locally with `meta_mcp_server` after `pip install -e .[server]`; the client then connects at http://127.0.0.1:8765/mcp. Its tools carry the 'mcp__meta-suite__*' prefix — deliberately distinct from the community 'mcp__meta-ads__*' prefix whose write tools are deny-listed in .claude/settings.json — so our sanctioned gated write tools are never caught by that deny-list. The role/token header for multi-user/hosted use is a later concern (see backlog mcp-role-based-access-tiers); local single-operator use needs no header.
